overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to social media analysis, disinformation, and computational social science. While the total follower count is significant, engagement varies across platforms. LinkedIn garners the highest individual post engagement, likely due to its professional focus.

contentThemes.description: Posts related to research findings, publications, and datasets in the field of computational social science.
Content focusing on the detection, analysis, and mitigation of disinformation and coordinated inauthentic behavior online.
Posts advertising job openings, research grants, and opportunities for PhD students.

recommendations: Cross-promote content across platforms to increase reach and engagement.
Use platform-specific hashtags to improve discoverability.
Engage more actively in conversations and respond to comments to foster a community.
Post visual content (images, videos) to improve engagement rates.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky posts see decent repost rates, suggesting audience interest in sharing content, but like counts could improve. The recent paper announcement performed well.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon engagement is the lowest, indicating a smaller or less active audience. Focus could be shifted to more engaging topics or formats.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the highest engagement in terms of likes and comments, indicating that professional announcements and job postings resonate well with the audience.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
